title: Turkey’s ‘Fighter Drone’ Teamed With M-346 Fighter-Trainer In Autonomy Trials
summary: Turkey conducted autonomy trials where its 'fighter drone' successfully teamed with an M-346 fighter-trainer aircraft. These demonstrations position Turkey among a select group of nations actively flight-testing advanced crewed-uncrewed teaming capabilities, crucial for future collaborative combat aircraft.

title: Executive order jumpstarts Pentagon’s quantum sensor projects
summary: New executive orders direct the Pentagon to develop three types of quantum sensors by 2028, support the Energy Department in building a quantum supercomputer, and advise on quantum hacking defenses. This initiative aims to accelerate quantum technology adoption for defense applications.

title: Inside The Making Of The New Air Force One
summary: L3Harris successfully converted a Qatari 747-8i into a VC-25B presidential transport, known as the new Air Force One, in just 10 months. This article details the complex and rapid transformation process of the highly specialized aircraft.

title: MARTAC and Intrepid Powerboats Announce Manufacturing Partnership to Scale Autonomous Surface Vessel Production
summary: MARTAC has partnered with Intrepid Powerboats to significantly scale up production of its Devil Ray Autonomous Surface Vessel (ASV) platforms. This collaboration aims to increase annual production to 200-300 vessels to meet growing demand from government, allied, and commercial customers.

title: Australia, Canada sign $1.75B agreement for Over the Horizon Radar system
summary: Australia and Canada have signed a $1.75 billion agreement for an Over the Horizon Radar (OTHR) system, marking Australia's largest defense export to date. This deal enhances long-range surveillance capabilities for both nations.

title: Vertical Aerospace's radical eVTOL design is taking shape for 2028
summary: An electric aircraft that takes off like a helicopter and cruises like a conventional plane just completed its first piloted flight with a new full-scale prototype. British company Vertical Aerospace is accelerating toward commercial certification, targeted for 2028. Continue Reading Category: Aircraft , Transport …
detail: British company Vertical Aerospace has successfully completed the first piloted flight of its full-scale eVTOL (electric Vertical Take-Off and Landing) prototype. This milestone brings the company closer to its goal of commercial certification by 2028. eVTOL aircraft are designed to take off and land vertically like helicopters but cruise efficiently like conventional planes, powered by electricity. This development is significant because it marks a tangible step towards realizing urban air mobility, potentially offering a quieter, more sustainable, and faster alternative for short-to-medium distance travel compared to traditional ground transport. The successful piloted flight demonstrates the viability of their design and contributes to the broader advancement of the eVTOL sector, which is a rapidly evolving area of aviation focused on creating 'flying taxis' and air shuttles. Challenges remain in areas like battery technology, air traffic control integration, and regulatory approval, but each successful flight test moves the industry closer to commercial reality.

title: Unassuming 1.6-oz EDC essential stashes serious survival capability
summary: After packaging full fire-starting capabilities neatly inside a 2-oz (57-g) capsule that doubled as a high-pitched whistle, Outdoor Element is now integrating that same spark-flicking capability into a more useful everyday tool: the common pen. Whether your 9-to-5 job involves a rare combination of manual record …
detail: Outdoor Element has introduced the 'Pen-metheus,' a new everyday carry (EDC) tool that integrates fire-starting capabilities into a common pen. This follows their previous innovation of a fire-starting whistle capsule. This product is notable as it exemplifies the growing trend of multi-functional EDC gear designed for practical utility and emergency preparedness in a compact form. For individuals who prioritize readiness or frequently engage in outdoor activities, having essential survival tools like a fire starter readily accessible, even in an unassuming item like a pen, can be crucial in unexpected situations. Everyday Carry (EDC) refers to items consistently carried for daily preparedness, often including multi-tools, flashlights, and now increasingly, discreet survival gadgets. The 'Pen-metheus' builds on the concept of combining convenience with preparedness, ensuring basic needs like fire (for warmth, cooking, or signaling) can be met without carrying bulky, specialized equipment, thereby enhancing personal safety and self-reliance.

title: Microsoft and Chevron plan one of the largest gas-powered data center projects in US
summary: Microsoft inked a 20-year power purchase agreement with Chevron, locking in decades of carbon emissions from a new natural gas power plant.
detail: Microsoft has entered into a 20-year power purchase agreement with Chevron to supply electricity to its data centers from a new natural gas power plant. This deal highlights a significant tension between the tech industry's rapid growth and its stated environmental commitments. While Microsoft has pledged to become carbon negative by 2030, securing a long-term agreement for fossil fuel-derived power for its energy-intensive data centers raises questions about the feasibility of meeting these ambitious targets. The partnership underscores the immense and continuously growing energy demands of data centers, particularly with the expansion of AI technologies, and the challenges associated with transitioning to 100% renewable energy sources at scale. Many tech companies face the dilemma of balancing reliable, continuous power supply with sustainability goals, often relying on natural gas as a 'bridge fuel' despite its carbon emissions. This specific collaboration with a major oil and gas company further complicates the narrative around corporate environmental responsibility.

title: AI is cursing renters with the promise of impossible homes
summary: Joyce, a native New Yorker, didn't think finding her first solo apartment in the city would be easy. But she also didn't think it'd be "hell." After looking at a lot of tiny, overpriced places she described as "shitholes," Joyce found her dream apartment: a reasonably priced studio in Manhattan. "It was big and airy, …
detail: Renters are increasingly encountering deceptive or entirely non-existent apartment listings online, often featuring AI-generated images and descriptions that promise impossible homes. This issue is causing significant frustration and wasted time for individuals navigating competitive housing markets, as illustrated by the anecdote of a renter finding a 'dream apartment' that likely does not exist as advertised. This problem highlights a growing misuse of artificial intelligence in online marketplaces, particularly in real estate. Generative AI tools make it easy for malicious actors to create highly convincing but fraudulent listings, exploiting the desperation of renters. This not only erodes trust in online platforms but also poses a risk of scams where individuals might pay for non-existent properties. The proliferation of such deceptive content presents a challenge for platform moderation and consumer protection, as the ease of creating realistic fake listings outpaces detection mechanisms, impacting the integrity of online rental markets.

title: Google invests in A24 to build AI movie tools
summary: Google's DeepMind AI lab is teaming up with A24 to develop new movie production technologies that aim to help future filmmakers "expand their storytelling possibilities." As part of this new research and development collaboration, The Wall Street Journal reports that Google is investing "around $75 million" into A24, …
detail: Google's DeepMind AI lab is collaborating with A24, an independent film studio, and investing approximately $75 million to develop new AI-powered tools for movie production. This partnership signifies a notable integration of artificial intelligence into the creative industries, specifically filmmaking. The stated goal is to 'expand storytelling possibilities,' suggesting that AI could assist with various aspects of film production, ranging from script development and visual effects to post-production and even marketing. Google's substantial investment and DeepMind's involvement indicate a serious commitment to exploring AI's transformative potential in cinema. This could lead to streamlining production processes, potentially reducing costs, or enabling entirely new forms of artistic expression. The collaboration between a leading AI research lab and a critically acclaimed film studio known for its distinctive films suggests a focus on innovative applications that aim to augment human creativity rather than merely automate existing tasks, marking a significant step in the evolution of filmmaking technology.

title: BitMine, Sharplink, Joe Lubin Back New Ethereum Research and Development Lab
summary: Some of Ethereum's largest holders are coming together to fund a new research and development lab aimed at boosting the network and ETH.
detail: Major Ethereum stakeholders, including mining firm BitMine, investment group Sharplink, and Ethereum co-founder Joe Lubin's ConsenSys, are jointly funding a new research and development lab. This initiative aims to enhance the Ethereum network and its native cryptocurrency, ETH. The move signifies a continued, foundational commitment to Ethereum's long-term development and stability. Dedicated R&D is crucial for addressing persistent challenges such as scalability, transaction costs, and security, which are vital for Ethereum's sustained growth and broader adoption. By investing resources into core infrastructure and innovation, these key players are demonstrating confidence in Ethereum's future as a leading decentralized platform, beyond merely holding its assets.

title: Morgan Stanley amends Ethereum, Solana ETFs to reveal record cheap fees
summary: ETF analyst Eric Balchunas says Morgan Stanley’s plan to charge 0.14% fees on two upcoming crypto ETFs makes them “the cheapest in [the] US and world.”
detail: Morgan Stanley has revised its filings for proposed Ethereum and Solana Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs), revealing exceptionally low management fees of 0.14%. An ETF analyst highlighted these as the cheapest crypto ETFs globally. This development is significant because low fees are a powerful competitive differentiator in the investment product market, making these ETFs highly attractive to both institutional and retail investors. The entry of a major financial institution like Morgan Stanley into the crypto ETF space, coupled with such aggressive pricing, signals increasing mainstream acceptance and institutionalization of cryptocurrencies as legitimate asset classes. This could drive substantial capital inflows into the Ethereum and Solana ecosystems, further integrating digital assets into traditional finance.

title: Franklin Templeton launches dedicated crypto division after closing 250 Digital acquisition
summary: The new unit arrives amid rapid growth in tokenized assets, with Franklin Templeton's onchain product suite expanding from roughly $768 million to more than $2.5 billion over the past year.
detail: Franklin Templeton, a prominent global asset manager, has established a dedicated crypto division following its acquisition of digital asset investment firm 250 Digital. This new unit will manage the company's expanding suite of on-chain products, which has seen substantial growth from approximately $768 million to over $2.5 billion in the past year. This strategic move by a traditional financial giant underscores the escalating institutional interest and long-term commitment to the digital asset space. Creating a specialized division validates the increasing importance of tokenized assets and blockchain technology within mainstream finance, potentially accelerating the integration of digital assets into conventional investment portfolios and fostering broader adoption of blockchain-based financial products.

title: Trump Orders Acceleration of Quantum Readiness as Bitcoin Faces Coming Risk
summary: President Donald Trump signed two executive orders aimed at expanding U.S. quantum computing capabilities and speeding the transition to quantum-resistant encryption.
detail: Former President Donald Trump signed two executive orders aimed at bolstering U.S. quantum computing capabilities and accelerating the transition to quantum-resistant encryption. This initiative addresses the long-term 'quantum threat' to current cryptographic standards, including those used to secure cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin. While present quantum computers are not powerful enough to compromise existing encryption, future advancements could theoretically break these systems. Accelerating quantum readiness involves investing in research for both quantum computing and post-quantum cryptography, which are crucial for national security and protecting sensitive digital information, including financial systems. This proactive approach highlights a national recognition of this future cybersecurity challenge and the imperative to develop robust defenses.

title: New York, Maryland and Utah to hold primaries with crypto PAC money hanging over voters
summary: Crypto-backed PACs disclosed spending more than $8 million on media to support candidates in three US states, as some Maryland Democrats have called on one candidate to reject “outside spending from crypto billionaires.”
detail: Crypto-backed Political Action Committees (PACs) have spent over $8 million on media campaigns to support candidates in upcoming primaries across New York, Maryland, and Utah. This significant spending highlights the cryptocurrency industry's growing influence in U.S. politics. Crypto PACs are actively engaging in elections to back candidates perceived as favorable to the industry's interests, particularly concerning regulation. The substantial financial investment indicates a concerted effort to shape policy and ensure a regulatory environment conducive to crypto innovation and growth. However, this influence has drawn criticism, with some Maryland Democrats calling on candidates to reject 'outside spending from crypto billionaires,' underscoring the contentious nature of special interest money in political campaigns.

title: Top 20 Largest Economies by GDP in the World (2026) - Business Model Analyst
source: "global economy OR inflation OR interest rate OR GDP when:2d" - Google News
summary: Top 20 Largest Economies by GDP in the World (2026) Business Model Analyst
detail: A report by Business Model Analyst projects the top 20 largest economies by Gross Domestic Product (GDP) for the year 2026. This projection indicates the anticipated economic size and output of nations in the near future. GDP is the total monetary value of all finished goods and services produced within a country's borders, serving as a key indicator of economic health and global influence. Such rankings matter because they highlight shifts in global economic power, identifying potential growth markets and investment destinations. For businesses, investors, and policymakers, understanding these future economic landscapes is crucial for strategic planning, trade agreements, and foreign policy decisions. The projections are based on current growth trends, demographic shifts, technological advancements, and policy trajectories, offering a forward-looking perspective on the world economy.

title: Larry Berman: The new era of the FOMC brings less transparency, more volatility - BNN Bloomberg
source: "global economy OR inflation OR interest rate OR GDP when:2d" - Google News
summary: Larry Berman: The new era of the FOMC brings less transparency, more volatility BNN Bloomberg
detail: Financial analyst Larry Berman suggests that the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC), the monetary policy-making body of the U.S. Federal Reserve, is entering a 'new era' characterized by reduced transparency, which could lead to increased market volatility. The FOMC's decisions on interest rates and money supply profoundly impact global financial markets. Historically, central banks have moved towards greater transparency through detailed communications to guide market expectations and reduce uncertainty. A departure from this trend, as suggested by Berman, means markets might have less clear signals about future policy actions. This lack of clarity could result in misinterpretations, sudden market reactions, and greater price swings across various asset classes, making it harder for investors and businesses to plan effectively and potentially increasing perceived economic risk.

title: OECD Report: India Remains World’s Fastest-Growing Major Economy - News On AIR
source: "global economy OR inflation OR interest rate OR GDP when:2d" - Google News
summary: OECD Report: India Remains World’s Fastest-Growing Major Economy News On AIR
detail: An OECD report confirms India's position as the world's fastest-growing major economy. This designation signifies India's robust economic performance and substantial growth potential compared to other large economies globally. The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) is an influential intergovernmental body, and its assessments carry significant weight. India's sustained rapid growth is crucial as it makes the country an increasingly attractive destination for foreign investment, strengthens its role in global trade, and impacts international supply chains. This growth is often attributed to its large domestic market, young demographic, increasing urbanization, and government-led initiatives aimed at boosting manufacturing and infrastructure, contrasting with the slower growth rates observed in many developed nations.

title: Will New US Fed Chair Kevin Warsh Raise Interest Rates This Year? - Morningstar
source: "global economy OR inflation OR interest rate OR GDP when:2d" - Google News
summary: Will New US Fed Chair Kevin Warsh Raise Interest Rates This Year? Morningstar
detail: This news item speculates whether a hypothetical new US Federal Reserve Chair, Kevin Warsh, would raise interest rates within the current year. The Federal Reserve Chair is a pivotal figure, wielding immense influence over monetary policy, which directly impacts the US and global economies. Interest rate decisions affect borrowing costs for consumers and businesses, inflation levels, and employment rates. A rate hike typically aims to curb inflation but can slow economic growth. Kevin Warsh, a former Fed Governor, was previously considered a candidate for the top role. The Fed's monetary policy decisions are guided by its dual mandate: achieving maximum employment and maintaining price stability. Factors like inflation data, unemployment figures, and global economic conditions are meticulously analyzed before any rate adjustments.

title: RBI keeps repo rate unchanged at 5.25% in first policy of FY27 after ceasefire in West Asia conflict - DD News
source: "global economy OR inflation OR interest rate OR GDP when:2d" - Google News
summary: RBI keeps repo rate unchanged at 5.25% in first policy of FY27 after ceasefire in West Asia conflict DD News
detail: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has maintained its repo rate at 5.25% in its first monetary policy review for the financial year 2027, a decision made following a ceasefire in the West Asia conflict. The repo rate is the benchmark interest rate at which commercial banks borrow funds from the RBI. Keeping it unchanged signals the RBI's assessment that the current monetary policy stance is appropriate for balancing inflation control with supporting economic growth. This stability suggests the RBI perceives current economic conditions as manageable, with no immediate need for tightening or loosening monetary policy. The mention of the West Asia ceasefire indicates that geopolitical stability, particularly its potential impact on global oil prices and supply chains, is a significant factor considered by the central bank in its policy deliberations, as such conflicts can fuel inflationary pressures.
